title: [Pikachu] Battle Guide
summary: A [light-ball] [lead] whose signature item doubles both Attack and SpA, turning the frail [Electric] mascot into a real [lead] threat through [fake-out] tempo.

recommendedItems.reason: Signature item doubling Attack and SpA

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Pikachu] is a frail [Electric] physical [lead] whose entire viability comes from [light-ball] — the signature item that doubles both Attack and SpA. 35/40/50 bulk and Speed 90 are nothing special on paper, but the doubled Attack means [volt-tackle] hits like a top-tier breaker, and [fake-out] gives it a [lead] slot the metagame actually respects.

## Strengths
[light-ball] is unique to [Pikachu] and effectively makes it a 2x damage attacker. [volt-tackle] becomes a one-shot move on most frail targets, and [play-rough] handles [Dark] and [Dragon] threats that would otherwise wall the [Electric] STAB. [fake-out] steals the first turn of tempo on [lead], and [lightning-rod] makes [Pikachu] an active redirect target for teammates that share an [Electric] matchup.

## Weaknesses & Answers
35/40/50 bulk is the lowest in the metagame, and any priority move or neutral hit threatens a KO. [Ground] is 2x and [earthquake] users completely ignore [Pikachu]. Speed 90 is also below most scarfed threats, so [Pikachu] loses the race after [fake-out] resolves. Lo[sing] [light-ball] to [knock-off] cripples the whole set instantly — be careful about trading items.

## Recommended Moveset
[volt-tackle] is the locked [Electric] STAB because [light-ball] is what makes the set viable. [play-rough] covers [Dark] and [Dragon] threats, and [knock-off] provides item removal for long-game value against walls. [fake-out] is the last slot for [lead] tempo — it often guarantees the first KO just by forcing a switch on turn one. Special variants with [thunderbolt] and [surf] exist but give up the [volt-tackle] ceiling.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[light-ball] is mandatory because it is the only reason this set exists. [focus-sash] is the survival alternative when [light-ball] is unavailable, trading damage for one guaranteed survival turn. Ability should be [lightning-rod] for ally redirection. Tera [Electric] maxes [volt-tackle] damage, and Tera [Ghost] flips the [fake-out] weakness defensively while adding immunity to [Normal] and [Fighting] moves.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Pikachu] is a [lead] that sets up its partners, so pair it with a setup sweeper that benefits from a guaranteed free turn. A [Ground] answer ([Flying] or [Grass] partner) covers the [earthquake] weakness, and a cleaner behind [Pikachu] converts its [volt-tackle] chip into KOs. [stealth-rock] support widens the OHKO ranges after the [lead] turn resolves.

## Play Patterns
Lead with [Pikachu], [fake-out] turn one, then [volt-tackle] or [play-rough] the weakened target. Use [knock-off] to strip walls of items, and Tera on any telegraphed [Ground] move. Never expose [Pikachu] to priority — one [focus-sash] break usually ends the set.
